commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Needs maintainer direction on whether the PR-shaped local-range surface should exist, plus contributor proof; ClawSweeper should not start an automated repair while proof and product direction are unresolved.; Needs attention: The diff introduces an advertised offline review mode without carrying over the existing offline review credential and network isolation safeguards.; Review finding: [P1] Reuse the hard... (sha=93b521e89df81f7da2c2be368bf0423e88e9707e)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `--local-range`, `--allow-closed`, `--body-file`, and `--additional-policy` to synthesize PR review context from a local git range and run advisory review before a GitHub PR exists.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review findings: source inspection shows the new `localRange` branch enters ordinary `reviewCommand`, and a scratch git rename check shows the name-status parser can produce an empty patch. I did not run the PR's review command because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs maintainer direction on whether the PR-shaped local-range surface should exist, plus contributor proof; ClawSweeper should not start an automated repair while proof and product direction are unresolved.

target.title: feat(review): offline `--local-range` mode for pre-submission review (#357 follow-on)
target.body:
## Summary

#357 added `review --local-only` for advisory review before submitting a PR — but it
still requires the issue/PR to already be **open on GitHub**: it fetches the item, diff,
body, and comments via the API (`--item-number`), and rejects closed items. So you can't
actually review your work *before* opening the PR, which is the stated goal.

This adds `--local-range`: review the local git range
(`merge-base(--base, HEAD)..HEAD`) with **no GitHub fetch**. The `Item` + `ItemContext`
are synthesized from `git` — the diff from `git diff`, the body from the commit message
(or `--body-file`) — so the full review (including the real-behavior-proof and Mantis
assessments) runs offline, before a PR exists, and on fork checkouts that the gh-fetch
`gitInfo` path rejects.

It also folds in three small, related advisory-review affordances that compose with it:

- `--allow-closed`: review closed/merged items (regression fixtures, hypothetical re-review).
- `--body-file <file>`: substitute the PR body in the prompt — to test the
  real-behavior-proof / Mantis decision against a hypothetical body, or to supply the body
  when reviewing a local range.
- `--additional-policy <file>`: layer a repo-specific policy file onto the review prompt.

## Changes

- `--local-range` (with `--base <ref>`, default `origin/main`): `buildLocalRangeReview()`
  builds a synthetic `Item` + `ItemContext` (`pullFiles` from `git diff`, body from the
  commit message) and `reviewCommand` branches three points — offline `gitInfo`
  (`{ mainSha, latestRelease: null }`, no fetch), the synthetic candidate, and the synthetic
  context — and suppresses the review-start comment.
- `--allow-closed`: relaxes the open-only selection gate in `selectCandidates`.
- `--body-file`: injects an authoritative PR-body section into the review prompt.
- `--additional-policy`: reads a policy file and layers it onto the review prompt.

## Validation

- `pnpm run check` (build, lint, unit + repair tests, changed + full coverage, format):
  passes — `fail 0`, format clean.
- Offline review of a real fork checkout (a 3-file, +113/-3 change) produced a valid
  Decision with **no GitHub access** — the diff was read from `git diff`, not the API:

  ```
  decision: keep_open · confidence: high
  realBehaviorProof.status:    missing       (the commit body carries no after-fix proof)
  telegramVisibleProof.status: not_needed
  mantisRecommendation.status: not_recommended
  ```

## Scope / notes

- This is advisory/offline only: `--local-range` implies no GitHub writes (no start
  comment, no posted review).
- Engine selection is intentionally out of scope for this PR.

Authored with Claude Code.
target.author: anagnorisis2peripeteia
